q: How does Nietzsche's will to power differ from Schopenhauer's will to live? Draw on the knowledge base for both thinkers and cite each in the source language.

judge.note: Ответ верно указывает, что у Шопенгауэра «воля к жизни» слепа и лежит в основе мирового страдания (но не раскрывает отрицание/отречение), а у Ницше «воля к власти» связана с ростом и не выводится из самосохранения; инверсия вывода относительно Шопенгауэра также отражена.

misattributions.claim: “Nietzsche grew out of Schopenhauer but inverted the conclusion: affirmation of life and self-overcoming instead of renunciation.”

confusion.note: The answer also attributes and contrasts items that match the key correctly, but the treatment implies a bidirectional inversion narrative (“grew out… inverted”) without citing it; the only explicit misattribution detectable is that the 'grew out/inverted conclusion' origin frame is not properly assigned.

cites.line: - Schopenhauer: schopenhauer/concepts/volya_k_zhizni — will as blind, foundational “воля к жизни” grounding the world and suffering
- Nietzsche: nietzsche/concepts/volya_k_vlasti — life is driven by “воля к власти,” not self-preservation, but the drive to grow, appropriate, and impose form
- Nietzsche: philosophers/ru/hub/nietzsche — “Воля к власти ≠ политическая «жажда власти»”; it is not just political domination

trace.final_text:
Nietzsche’s and Schopenhauer’s views are opposed along the axis the notes explicitly name: **“Воля к жизни → воля к власти.”** The knowledge base says that for Schopenhauer, will is **blind** and is the metaphysical core of the world as **“воля к жизни”**; for Nietzsche, what drives the living is **not self-preservation** but the drive to **manifest strength, grow, appropriate, and impose form**.

So the difference is:

- **Schopenhauer:** will is a blind, fundamental life-drive; the world is grounded in **“воля к жизни”**, and this underlies suffering.
- **Nietzsche:** will is reinterpreted as **“воля к власти”**; life is not primarily about preserving itself, but about expanding power, growth, appropriation, and form-giving.

The notes also warn not to flatten Nietzsche’s term into a merely political idea: **“Воля к власти ≠ политическая «жажда власти»”**. So it is broader than domination over others; it is a general principle of life’s strengthening and self-overcoming.

Directly grounded in the retrieved material, that is the contrast the knowledge base gives.

SOURCES

- Schopenhauer: schopenhauer/concepts/volya_k_zhizni — will as blind, foundational “воля к жизни” grounding the world and suffering
- Nietzsche: nietzsche/concepts/volya_k_vlasti — life is driven by “воля к власти,” not self-preservation, but the drive to grow, appropriate, and impose form
- Nietzsche: philosophers/ru/hub/nietzsche — “Воля к власти ≠ политическая «жажда власти»”; it is not just political domination
